verdict: The single-source machinery (names_index.yaml + descriptor_registry mirror + rename executor + 4 readers) is unusually well-designed, but its BINDING is loose, so definitions still diverge. Two files each self-declare the naming authority and contradict each other on the attribute roster (glossary: 7 attrs, Cognition/Presence/Spirit canonical; descriptor_registry/names_index: 9 attrs, those are aliases). The registry deprecates a concept (resonance_style) that runs live and central as npc_behavior's Resonant Style. MS/RS drift survives its own registered lint because enforcement is warn-tier, not block. glossary is a third authority outside the mirror gate entirely. Combat Pool, by contrast, is genuinely reconciled — do not report it as divergent. Cohesiveness: MODERATE-WEAK.
key_points: names_index.yaml is the correct every-rule-lives-once home and is well-built; the failure is enforcement/binding, not architecture.
Attribute roster diverges across the two naming authorities: glossary Part One (7 attrs) vs descriptor_registry+names_index (9 attrs), disagreeing on both count and which name is canonical.

descriptor_registry DEPRECATES resonance_style ('superseded by contest_style, retired 2026-06-06') while npc_behavior_v30 §1.3 runs Resonant Style as the live gating mechanic for all NPC contest AI — a registry-vs-mechanism contradiction a Godot importer would trip on.

MS/RS: names_index registers the rename (clock.mending_stability, legacy Rendering Stability) but only at enforce:warn, so params/threadwork.md still reads RS canonically — the lint that should catch it is non-blocking.

glossary.md self-claims naming authority yet is NOT in the ci_names_consistency mirror set (only descriptor_registry + proper_noun_registry are) — the un-mirrored third authority is the systemic root of the roster drift.
Enforcement is almost entirely warn-tier: only world.solmund (Galbados) is enforce:block; every attribute/stat/clock/mechanic is warn, so Godot-load-bearing names have no hard gate.
Only combat_engine_v1.json is a typed export; 26 modules are prose-only, so every non-combat value crossing into Godot is hand-transcribed with no round-trip check (CLAUDE.md §5).

Combat Pool is VERIFIED reconciled: core.md line 161 and module_contracts line 801 agree on max(5,History+6); the struck Agi×2 form survives only in quarantined/known-stale carriers — not a live divergence.
conviction_track_v1 (personal Scars) vs conviction_track_v30 (territory Piety) share a stem whose version suffix falsely reads as supersession — already miscited by mechanics_index.
Fix path: fold glossary tables into the mirror gate (or generate them from names_index), flip attribute/MS-RS entries warn->block, and reconcile resonance_style against live Resonant Style.
Terminological cohesiveness grade: MODERATE-WEAK — sound design, loose binding.
mass_combat Command formula (Presence+Cognition, PP-232) is written against LEGACY attribute names, so roster churn silently propagates into live formula prose.

findings.claim: glossary.md Part One (7 attributes; Cognition/Presence/Spirit as canonical) and descriptor_registry.yaml+names_index.yaml (9 attributes; those three demoted to aliases of Acuity/Charisma/Will) each self-declare the naming authority and directly contradict each other on attribute count and canonical names.
findings.cite: references/glossary.md §Part One Core Attributes vs references/descriptor_registry.yaml §attributes + references/names_index.yaml attr.*

findings.claim: descriptor_registry marks resonance_style deprecated/superseded-by-contest_style/retired, but npc_behavior_v30 §1.3 runs Resonant Style as the live, central contest-targeting mechanic for every named NPC — the registry declares a load-bearing mechanic dead.
findings.cite: references/descriptor_registry.yaml §deprecated (resonance_style, line 100) vs designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §1.3 Resonant Style Taxonomy

findings.claim: ci_names_consistency mirror-checks descriptor_registry and proper_noun_registry against names_index but NOT glossary.md, so the glossary — which self-claims 'canonical reference for all term expansions' — sits outside the single-source gate and drifts freely, which is why the attribute roster contradiction persists.
findings.cite: references/names_index.yaml header MIRRORS block; references/glossary.md line 4 authority claim

findings.claim: The MS/RS rename is registered in names_index (clock.mending_stability, legacy Rendering Stability) but only at enforce:warn, so params/threadwork.md still reads RS as canonical throughout — the single-source home exists yet its own drift-lint is non-blocking and never forced the sweep.
findings.cite: references/names_index.yaml clock.mending_stability (enforce: warn); params/threadwork.md §'RS Track (PP-603 — canonical)'
findings.tracked_ref: ED-731/ED-772 (threadwork dossier ms-rs-split-live); new scope: the registered lint is warn-tier so drift survives the machinery

findings.claim: references/engine_params/ holds only combat_engine_v1.json; the other 26 modules are prose-only, so every non-combat value crossing into Godot is hand-transcribed with no round-trip CI check — the definitional pipeline is single-sourced-and-typed for exactly one subsystem.
findings.tracked_ref: CLAUDE.md §5 typed engine-params gap (ED-1052 first artifact)

findings.claim: In names_index only world.solmund is enforce:block; every attribute, faction/settlement stat, clock (including MS/RS) and mechanic rename is enforce:warn, so the Godot-load-bearing definition names have no hard gate and drift persists by construction.
findings.cite: references/names_index.yaml (enforce fields across all non-solmund entries)

findings.claim: Contrary to CLAUDE.md §5's 'defined three ways', Combat Pool is now single-sourced at both live sites (params/core.md line 161 and module_contracts line 801 both = max(5,History+6)); the struck (Agility×2)+History+3 form survives only in quarantined values_master and enumerated known-stale carriers (mechanics_index, sim, data_serialization), so it is not a live divergence.
findings.tracked_ref: ED-1084 propagation; module_contracts line 841 stale-carrier list

findings.claim: conviction_track_v1.md (personal per-Conviction Scars) and conviction_track_v30.md (per-territory Piety BG stat) share a stem whose version suffix falsely reads as supersession rather than 'different mechanic', a schema hazard that already caused mechanics_index to cite the wrong file.
findings.cite: designs/personal/conviction_track_v1.md and designs/scene/conviction_track_v30.md (both extant); references/glossary.md §Part Three Piety Track
